posts.title: Voice AI in production: a short checklist for operators
posts.description: Before you scale outbound or inbound voice automation, use this checklist to cover disclosures, fallbacks, analytics, and handoff — without drowning in prompt tweaks.
posts.author: Voiceable
posts.body:
Operators ship voice AI faster when they treat agents like **systems**, not demos. Use this lightweight checklist before you scale traffic.

## 1. Purpose and boundaries

| Question | Why it matters |
|----------|----------------|
| What job does the agent own end-to-end? | Scope creep breaks transcripts and KPIs. |
| When must it escalate to a human? | Clear rules reduce liability and angry callers. |

## 2. Disclosure and consent

State clearly that the caller is speaking with an **automated system** where regulations or carriers expect it. Keep the line short and repeatable.

## 3. Measurable outcomes

Track **completion**, **escalation rate**, and **cost per successful outcome** — not just call volume. Tie definitions to business vocabulary (booked meeting, qualified lead, ticket opened).

## 4. Knowledge and tools

Ground answers in **approved sources** and restrict tools to the smallest set that achieves the job. Fewer tools means fewer failure modes.

---

Voiceable helps teams define outcomes and workflows without writing prompts by hand. Learn more about [Voiceable](/) or jump to [pricing](/pricing).

posts.title: Welcome to the Voiceable blog
posts.description: Product updates, voice AI best practices, and how teams ship reliable agents without prompt engineering — from the Voiceable team.
posts.author: Voiceable
posts.body:
We're building **Voiceable** so you can deploy AI voice agents that handle real calls — qualification, scheduling, and handoffs — without brittle prompt stacks.

On this blog we'll share:

- **Shipping patterns** that keep agents reliable in production
- **Integration and workflow** notes as we expand the platform
- **Concrete guides** for operators moving from experiments to always-on voice

Ready to try it? See [pricing](/pricing) or [create an account](/sign-up).
